description: Two sound-but-lingering retro notes from fz-rh2.21: (1) Scheduler::rebased marks are only removed when the marked job next CONCLUDES; a job that never reruns keeps its mark forever (bounded by job count, no correctness impact — the mark only changes that job's own next conclude). (2) union_reads only ever grows a blocked job's subscription set; reads it no longer performs are dropped only at the next wait-free conclusion, so a long-blocked job can be woken by facts it stopped caring about (bounded wake amplification, no correctness impact). Neither blocks anything; address only with a holistic look at scheduler lifecycle — do not bolt on ad-hoc pruning.
title: Compiler2 fact-engine hygiene: prune rebased marks and shrink waiting subscriptions
